title: Chosen Before You Could Choose
reading:
Ephesians 1:3-14 is one of the densest passages of grace in all of Scripture. Paul barely pauses for breath — and what he is describing, relentlessly, is the depth of God's initiative toward you.

Before the foundation of the world, He chose you. Before you existed, He planned for you to be holy and blameless before Him. He predestined you to adoption. He made you accepted in the Beloved. He sealed you with the Holy Spirit as a guarantee — not a possibility, not a hope, a guarantee — of your inheritance.

None of this was in response to your performance. It was planned before your performance was even possible.

Zephaniah 3:17 adds something remarkable to this picture: the Lord your God rejoices over you with gladness. He quiets you with His love. He rejoices over you with singing. This is not the image of a God who merely tolerates you. It is the image of a God who delights in you.

People pleasing finds its root in believing that God is reluctant — that His approval is conditional and uncertain. Ephesians 1 and Zephaniah 3 say otherwise. He sealed you. He sings over you.
reflection: Do you believe that God rejoices over you — not your best moments, but you? What does it mean to be sealed by the Holy Spirit as a guarantee?
abide: Father, let me receive what You have already given. You chose me before I could choose You. Let that truth go deeper than my head today.
